pi_items.source: Code analysis: task.json metadata, verifier.py line 31, setup_task.sh and export_result.sh all reference this name consistently
Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 10 creates PROJECTS array with these 3 as first elements; task description line 5 specifies these exact projects to add to working set
Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 10 creates PROJECTS array with these 2 as last elements; task description line 5 explicitly states 'Do NOT add' these projects

Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 6 sets WORKSPACE_DIR=/home/ga/eclipse-workspace, line 65 uses $WORKSPACE_DIR/.metadata/.plugins/org.eclipse.ui.workbench/workingsets.xml; export_result.sh line 9-10 confirms same path. Web search confirmed this is Eclipse's standard working sets configuration file location.

privileged_info_summary: The task 'organize_working_sets@1' operates on synthetic data. The metadata correctly identifies the target working set name as 'ActiveDoseDev', requires three synthetic Java projects (DoseEngine, DoseUI, DoseTests) to be added to the working set, and excludes two other synthetic projects (Legacy-Converter, ThirdParty-Utils). The working set configuration file path is correctly specified as /home/ga/eclipse-workspace/.metadata/.plugins/org.eclipse.ui.workbench/workingsets.xml, which follows Eclipse IDE's standard file structure for storing working set definitions. All metadata claims are internally consistent with the task setup and verification scripts.
